recipes.title: Steak Fries with Green Peppercorn Sauce 🥩🍟
steps: Pat the sirloin steaks dry and season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper.

Heat the oil in a pan over high heat and sear the steaks 2–3 min per side. Add the butter, crushed garlic, thyme and rosemary, and spoon the foaming butter over the steaks for another minute. Rest for 5 min before slicing.
For the sauce, soften the finely chopped shallot in the same pan. Pour in the white wine and let it reduce by half.
Stir in the cream, green peppercorns, beef stock concentrate, soy sauce, mustard and salt. Simmer, stirring, until the sauce coats the back of a spoon.
Cut the baking potatoes into fries and fry in hot oil until deep golden and crisp. Drain on paper towel.
Toss the hot fries with the chopped parsley, grated Parmesan, grated garlic and a pinch of salt.
Serve the sliced steak with the fries and spoon the green peppercorn sauce over the top.
recipes.notes: Method added from the ingredient list — tweak the timings to how you like your steak 💕

recipes.title: Banana Chocolate Chip Muffins 🍌🧁

ingredients.name: sour cream or whole milk Greek yogurt, room temp (60g)
semi-sweet chocolate chips (250g), + more for topping

steps: In a mixing bowl, roughly mash the bananas. Add the brown sugar and granulated sugar and mix well to combine.
Crack in the egg and mix for 2–3 min until the mixture turns a light, pale color.
Add the melted butter, vanilla bean paste and sour cream, and mix until well combined and blended.
In a separate bowl, mix the dry ingredients: flour, baking powder, baking soda, salt and cinnamon.
Add the dry ingredients to the wet batter and gently fold in. Add the chocolate chips and fold just to combine — do not over mix.
Let the batter sit at room temperature for 1 hour, undisturbed, before baking.
Scoop into a lined muffin tin, top with extra chocolate chips, and bake until a toothpick comes out clean (about 18–22 min at 350°F / 375°F for taller tops).
recipes.notes: The original recipe didn't give a bake time/temp — 350–375°F for ~18–22 min is a safe starting point. Resting the batter 1 hr makes them extra tender 🧁

recipes.title: Banana Bread

ingredients.name: unsalted butter (8 Tbsp), at room temperature

steps: Preheat the oven to 350°F. Grease and flour a bread loaf pan (9.25 long x 5.25 wide x 2.75 deep).
Lightly roast walnuts on a skillet, continuously stirring so they won't burn. Coarsely chop and cool to room temperature.
In a mixing bowl, cream together the softened butter and sugar (or honey if using honey).
Mash bananas with a fork until the consistency of chunky applesauce and add them to the batter along with the eggs, mixing until blended.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the flour, baking soda and salt, then add to the batter.
Add the vanilla extract and mix in the chopped walnuts and chocolate chips. Pour into the prepared loaf pan.
Bake at 350°F for 55–60 min, or until a toothpick inserted into the center comes out clean. Let the banana bread rest for 10 min before transferring to a wire rack to cool.
recipes.notes: Prep 10 min, bake ~1 hour. The sugar can be swapped for honey 🍯

recipes.title: Arsenio's Crunch Cookie 🍪
steps: Mix soft butter, sugar, brown sugar, and salt.
Add eggs + vanilla, mix until smooth.
Fold in flour + baking soda until just combined.
Add chocolate chips.
Scoop onto a baking tray — makes 6 massive cookies.
Bake at 350°F for 12 minutes.
recipes.notes: the trick: spin them with a bowl or cup right out of the oven to shape… then let them rest 😮‍🔥

diary.note: The taste was a 10/10 but the baking time needed more. It was still pretty soft and raw in the middle. We should divide it into 4 minimum next time.

Bunu Mustafa'nın doğum günü için yaptım. İlk deneme için gerçekten iyi olduğunu düşünüyorum, ancak Mustafa biraz daha tatlı olabileceğini söyledi. Yine de Okan neredeyse hepsini bitirdi. Bu yüzden 4 yıldız veriyorum.
